project.vision: A distinctive 1990s low-poly browser crime drama built around Ashfall City, with authored open-world exploration, memorable characters, cinematic missions, vehicles, and reliable browser performance.
project.creativeBoundary: Use the open-world crime genre as a design reference, but do not copy GTA names, dialogue, missions, branding, artwork, UI layouts, music, characters, or story beats.

orchestrator.implementationPolicy: The orchestrator plans, reviews dependencies, and dispatches bounded work. Implementation, asset acquisition, editing, and QA belong to medium-effort workers in dedicated worktrees.

selection: Read this entire file and AGENTS.md.
Inspect current Codex task status, Git worktrees, branches, and main before dispatching.
Do not duplicate a task whose task ID appears in an active task title, branch name, worktree path, commit, or completed roadmap entry.
Select only tasks whose dependencies are completed and whose status is ready.
Fill safe capacity toward four active workers and use a fifth only for an independent task with satisfied dependencies and no shared ownership conflict.
Balance architecture, gameplay, content, visual/assets, and test-performance work rather than assigning multiple workers to the same foundational contract.
Do not start more work when five workers are active or more than six worktrees including main are registered.
workerRules: Create or reuse one dedicated worker task and worktree per bounded task.
Name branches worker/<task-id>-<short-name> and include the task ID in the task title.
Start from the current origin/main unless the task explicitly depends on an unmerged worker.

Begin every worker prompt with an exact cd to its assigned /Users/jschuler/Projects/vanta-city-worktrees/<name> path, then verify git rev-parse --show-toplevel, git branch --show-current, git rev-parse HEAD, and git status --short --branch before any read or edit.
Stop without editing if the verified repository root is not the assigned Vanta City worktree or if the starting state differs from the orchestrator's assignment.
Workers must not merge or push main.
Workers must commit focused changes, leave worktrees clean, and report checks, screenshots, architectural decisions, provenance, performance measurements, and risks.
Visual workers must inspect screenshots and the browser console. Runtime changes must use existing public lifecycle, input, camera, collision, asset, game-state, debug, and browser-test contracts.

If a suitable production-intended asset cannot be found with verified usage rights, block the task instead of shipping a placeholder. Synthetic assets remain allowed only in explicit failure-path tests.
integrationRules: Review commit history, changed files, APIs, tests, asset assumptions, performance impact, and overlap before merging.
Prefer one authoritative system over adapters or duplicate browser listeners.
Never merge an active, dirty, uncommitted, failing, or conceptually incompatible worker.
Run formatting, linting, type-checking, unit tests, relevant asset validators, production build, bundle size, targeted browser tests, and smoke tests.
Skip the complete E2E suite during hourly integration. Reserve it for explicit release milestones or a separately approved scheduled gate.
Commit integration corrections separately, update task status and metrics in this file, push main, and report exactly what changed.
Never delete a dirty or active worktree. Preserve worker branches when cleaning completed worktrees.
cleanupRules: Run only after the hourly integrator's scheduled window and re-audit task state immediately before deletion.
Remove only registered worktrees below /Users/jschuler/Projects/vanta-city-worktrees whose Git status is completely clean and whose owning Codex task is inactive or completed.
Require evidence that the worker result is integrated into origin/main through ancestry, patch equivalence, or a completed roadmap entry with an integration commit before removal.
Never remove /Users/jschuler/Projects/vanta-city, a worktree used by an active process, a dirty worktree, an uncommitted branch, or a worktree with unresolved validation or integration status.
Use git worktree remove and git worktree prune; preserve worker branches.
Do not delete unregistered directories automatically. Report orphan directories, their size, age, and contents for later reviewed cleanup.
Report registered worktree count, active worker count, reclaimed bytes, remaining disk use, and every skipped path with its reason.

memory.policy: Memory is a ceiling, not a target. Exceed 900 MB only with a recorded benchmark, a documented reason, no leak trend across three load/unload cycles, and explicit roadmap approval.

rules: Apply the growth percentage once per map-expansion milestone, not once per unrelated feature run.
Describe each expansion in an authored machine-readable map plan before building it.
Keep buildings on the outer edge of sidewalks with continuous pedestrian clearance, readable entrances, authored collision, camera recovery space, and traffic-safe lanes.
Add curved streets through shared spline-based road, lane, collision, traffic, and minimap geometry rather than visual-only bends.
Load only nearby sectors during gameplay and provide a separate full-map representation for the map UI.
Every expansion must record area, buildings, sectors, draw calls, triangles, memory, FPS, collision coverage, and screenshot paths.

secretPolicy.worktreeAccess: Approved provider workers may read required values from the absolute credential file because ignored files are not copied into Git worktrees.
prohibitions: Do not copy the credential file or its values into a worktree.
Do not print, log, serialize, stage, commit, screenshot, or include secret values in prompts, reports, URLs, or test artifacts.
Do not expose provider keys to browser bundles, runtime network requests, client storage, debug snapshots, or error messages.

requirements: Use only official OpenAI API endpoints from server-side or development scripts.
Never print, persist, transmit elsewhere, expose to browser code, or commit the API key.
Store accepted images locally with prompt, model, request metadata, generation date, hash, and asset purpose.
Inspect every candidate for originality, visual fit, text artifacts, protected characters, brands, and unintended franchise resemblance before acceptance.
Use only official ElevenLabs API endpoints from development scripts; runtime playback uses accepted local audio files.
Never print, persist, transmit elsewhere, expose to browser code, or commit either environment value.
Record character-cost, request ID, model, output format, prompt or script, generation date, hash, and asset purpose for accepted files.
Generate no character dialogue voice-over. The configured voice is reserved for the in-world car radio host.
Do not issue radio-host TTS requests until the configured voice returns successfully from the authenticated voice metadata endpoint.
Verify the source page and license before downloading.
Store reviewed runtime assets locally with source URL, author, license, retrieval date, hash, scale, forward axis, animation list, and modification notes.
Use production-intended assets in visual and browser acceptance tests; do not approve placeholder geometry as feature completion.
Run characters and animated NPCs through the animation lab with persistent transform controls and screenshots.
Do not copy real brands, protected characters, famous buildings, GTA material, or unclear-license marketplace assets.

uiDesign.authority: One shared HUD layout owns screen-space zones, safe-area behavior, stacking, region collisions, and state-driven visibility. Feature UI observes authoritative public snapshots and events without owning simulation truth.
taskTriggers: src/ui or shared UI style changes
dialogue, cinematic, mission, map, menu, loading, title, pause, death, or vehicle presentation
input focus, pointer, accessibility, responsive, typography, icon, or motion changes
rules: Use the vanta-ui-art-director skill for design briefs and final visual review before implementation tasks that match a trigger.
Use xhigh consultation for design judgment and medium workers in dedicated worktrees for implementation and QA.
Prefer shared semantic tokens and layout zones over component-local global positioning, colors, z-indexes, fonts, breakpoints, and animation curves.
Review deterministic UI composition-lab and live-game screenshots over bright, dark, and visually noisy backgrounds at required viewports.

Do not accept snapshot updates without a recorded design reason, inaccessible controls, placeholder interface art, unlicensed fonts or icons, private runtime state access, or copied franchise presentation.

productRules.missionPresentation: World triggers and objectives use a shared mission system with authored map/world highlights and event hooks.
productRules.cinematicPresentation: Cinematics use the camera system, subtitle dialogue, skip confirmation, exact gameplay restoration, and no voice-over requirement.
productRules.vehiclePresentation: Vehicles use one enter-drive-exit ownership flow with game-state, camera, collision, traffic, and input integration inspired by genre conventions but implemented originally.
productRules.fullMapPresentation: A dedicated map action opens a full-world sector map without replacing the existing minimap.
productRules.titleAndLoadingPresentation: Create an original 1990s Ashfall title screen and locally stored rotating loading artwork that reflects completed story locations and characters. GPT Image 2 generation is authorized within assetPolicy request limits.
productRules.audioPresentation: Use ElevenLabs for an original instrumental theme and radio pipeline within assetPolicy request limits. Reserve the configured voice for the car radio host, generate no character dialogue voice-over, and require voice-access validation before radio-host TTS.

goals: Keep unit tests under ten seconds on the reference machine.
Keep hourly targeted browser validation under three minutes.
Use deterministic public snapshots and events instead of arbitrary sleeps.
Run screenshot-heavy labs only for changed visual systems.
Profile before increasing Playwright workers and preserve WebGL isolation.

continuousDiscovery.goal: Keep the roadmap useful after the initial queue is exhausted by converting user direction and reproducible local playtest evidence into small, reviewable tasks.

queuePolicy.policy: When fewer than six dependency-safe tasks are ready, or a critical reproducible issue is found, the xhigh orchestrator may select proposals but must dispatch a medium-effort backlog-curation worker in a dedicated worktree to edit this file. The hourly integrator reviews and merges those edits. The orchestrator never edits main itself.

evidenceSources: browser console and uncaught runtime errors
FPS, frame-time, memory, streaming, and disposal telemetry
taskRequirements: Give every task a stable ID, title, source, priority, bounded scope, owner, dependencies, acceptance criteria, validation, risks, and a duplicate key.
Include report artifact paths and deterministic reproduction steps for defect tasks.
Check proposed work against task IDs, duplicate keys, roadmap entries, active Codex tasks, worktrees, branches, commits, and recent integration history before promotion.
Prefer fixing a root contract or extending an existing task over creating adjacent duplicates.

Do not create autonomous tasks that change the product vision, authorize spending, handle credentials, weaken safety or licensing, introduce mature content, or expand into unapproved legal or external-service commitments; request user direction instead.
Do not promote purely aesthetic observations without screenshots, an articulated design goal, and objective acceptance criteria.

requiredArtifacts: Playwright video for each completed seed
screenshots at state transitions and anomalies
JSON action timeline with seed and reproduction commands
browser console, page errors, failed requests, and uncaught exceptions
public game-state, camera, player, mission, interaction, combat, vehicle, streaming, FPS, frame-time, and memory diagnostics when available
artifact manifest with byte sizes, hashes, timestamps, Git SHA, viewport, browser version, and pass/fail summary
rules: Run against clean origin/main using a production build or the repository's documented production-preview path.
Use public inputs and debug/test snapshots for observation; do not mutate private runtime fields to manufacture progress.
Exercise only capabilities currently registered by the game and record skipped actions as unavailable rather than failing the whole run.
Do not call paid APIs, access secrets, download assets, commit artifacts, edit source, create worktrees, merge, or push.
Keep generated videos, screenshots, traces, and raw diagnostics ignored by Git. Prune only files beneath reports/playbot according to this retention policy.
The recorder supplements deterministic tests; its output never replaces unit, targeted browser, visual, smoke, or release gates.

roadmap.title: Boot directly as Casual
acceptance: Boot bypasses the character picker and selects casual through the existing character selection state.
The character registry, fallback loading, debug switching, and tests remain available.
No player simulation is recreated solely to choose the visual.

roadmap.title: Author the Ashfall 1990s narrative bible
acceptance: Define the original setting, themes, timeline, factions, Rook/Casual identity, supporting cast, act structure, and first six mission premises.
Provide structured speaker, location, mission, and story-beat IDs suitable for runtime definitions.
Avoid GTA-specific names, copied story beats, and copyrighted dialogue.

roadmap.title: Build sector streaming and performance telemetry
acceptance: Define authored world sectors and deterministic load/unload ownership using the existing asset and level lifecycle.
Expose sector, draw-call, triangle, texture, memory-proxy, frame-time, and disposal diagnostics.
Prove three sector load/unload cycles without increasing retained scene objects or asset references.

roadmap.title: Expand Ashfall by 25 percent with the first curved street
acceptance: Increase walkable playable area to 3763–4077 square metres and record the exact result.
Add one authored curved road corridor whose visuals, lanes, collision, traffic paths, and minimap share spline-derived geometry.
Add production buildings at outer sidewalk edges without reducing pedestrian clearance.
Meet FPS, memory, collision, camera, and visual screenshot gates.

roadmap.title: Acquire and validate the first expanded pedestrian cast
acceptance: Add at least four license-verified low-poly NPC models that fit Ashfall's art direction.
Each NPC has production idle and interaction animation coverage, documented provenance, correct scale/forward direction, disposal tests, and animation-lab controls.
No placeholder model is accepted as the completed visual.

roadmap.title: Create the authoritative mission and objective system
acceptance: Implement mission definitions, prerequisites, objective state, world triggers, highlighted world/map targets, event hooks, persistence-ready snapshots, cancellation, completion, debug controls, and disposal.
Mission definitions reference entity, location, dialogue, cinematic, and speaker IDs instead of loaded objects.
Add a production first-mission skeleton from the story bible without placeholder mission text.

roadmap.title: Implement enter, drive, and exit vehicle foundation
acceptance: Use a production traffic vehicle asset for enter, seated ownership, acceleration, braking, reverse, steering, collision, camera, exit placement, pause, and recovery.
Player simulation and vehicle simulation have explicit ownership transfer without duplicate input listeners.
Driving is responsive and original, with genre-appropriate handling but no copied GTA values or UI.

roadmap.title: Add a full-world map screen
acceptance: A named map action opens a pause-safe full-sector map distinct from the minimap.
It shows authored roads, curves, buildings, mission highlights, player position, locations, zoom, pan, accessibility labels, and clean input ownership.
Opening and closing restores the exact prior gameplay state and camera ownership.

roadmap.title: Create the cinematic sequence system
acceptance: Cinematic definitions request camera shots through the camera system and render subtitle dialogue through shared dialogue presentation.
Skip input opens a confirmation prompt; confirm exits cleanly and cancel resumes the sequence.
Completion and skipping restore game state, movement, interaction, camera, HUD, and input ownership exactly.
Add one production opening scene based on the story bible and validate desktop/narrow screenshots.

roadmap.title: Ship the first complete story mission
acceptance: A concrete story mission connects an opening cinematic, world objective highlights, dialogue, on-foot movement, vehicle use, completion, reward, failure/retry, and post-mission world state.
All text and character beats come from the approved story bible.
The complete browser path uses production-intended assets and records console, FPS, memory, and screenshots.

roadmap.title: Create the original 1990s title and loading presentation
acceptance: Create an original Ashfall title screen, typography system, and locally stored loading artwork based on shipped characters and locations.

Use the configured OpenAI API only through bounded development-time GPT Image 2 generation; store accepted art locally with complete prompt/model/hash provenance and never expose the key to browser runtime.
Loading transitions reflect real sector or asset readiness and do not conceal hangs.
No GTA artwork, logo composition, names, or music is copied.

roadmap.title: Create the theme and radio audio pipeline
knownBlockers: The configured radio voice ID is well formed but is not currently accessible to the configured ElevenLabs account; authenticated voice lookup returns voice_not_found.
Radio-host TTS remains blocked until the voice is added to the account library or ELEVENLABS_RADIOGUY_VOICE_ID is replaced with an accessible voice.
acceptance: Implement the local audio catalog/playback pipeline and generate at most two original instrumental theme candidates through the official ElevenLabs Music API with recorded provenance and API parameters.

Validate the configured radio voice through the authenticated metadata endpoint before generating up to three bounded radio-host TTS candidates; block only the radio-host generation subtask if access still fails.
Implement local runtime playback, streaming/disposal rules, volume/accessibility controls, and radio interruption behavior.
Do not generate character dialogue voice-over in this milestone.

roadmap.title: Establish the Ashfall UI design system and composition lab
roadmap.designBrief: Create a tracked Ashfall UI foundation brief before implementation using the vanta-ui-art-director contract.
visualEvidence: live gameplay desktop, narrow, and ultrawide screenshots
bright daytime, dark nighttime, and visually noisy background stress screenshots
acceptance: Audit the existing HUD, menus, dialogue, overlays, styles, game-state presentation, input/focus ownership, screenshots, and public snapshots before changing architecture.

Define an original 1990s Ashfall design language and semantic tokens for typography, color, spacing, layers, panels, icons, motion, safe areas, contrast, and responsive behavior without copying another game's recognizable interface.

Create one authoritative screen-space HUD layout with semantic zones for player status, navigation, loadout, interaction, objectives, notifications, conversation, modal, presentation, and world indicators; migrate existing UI without duplicating state or listeners.

Build a deterministic UI composition lab that renders available exploration, combat, dialogue, mission, driving, pause/map, loading, death, failure, warning, unavailable, and restoration states through public presentation fixtures on real game backgrounds.
Meet keyboard focus, accessible naming, live-region, non-color cue, text enlargement, safe-area, narrow, ultrawide, and reduced-motion requirements.

Inspect console output and the required screenshot matrix manually, add intentional visual baselines and behavioral tests, document every shared design decision, and report unavailable future-state dependencies rather than shipping placeholders.

roadmap.title: Build the local recorded playtest bot
acceptance: Add a bounded npm playtest:bot command that runs deterministic critical-path and seeded-exploration sessions against a local production build without modifying source or runtime private fields.

Record Playwright video, transition and anomaly screenshots, an action timeline, browser errors, failed requests, game diagnostics, FPS, frame-time, memory, Git SHA, seed, and exact reproduction commands into ignored reports/playbot run directories.

Exercise available boot, movement, interaction, dialogue, combat, equipment, pickup, mission, vehicle, map, pause, and restoration capabilities through public inputs; explicitly report capabilities that are not yet registered.
Enforce five-minute, two-seed, 500 MB per-run, 1 GB total, five-run, and seven-day retention bounds without deleting anything outside reports/playbot.
Produce latest.json and a concise human-readable summary whose findings can be promoted into nonduplicate roadmap tasks.
Pass two repeated local runs without uncaught bot failures and document why recorded exploration complements rather than replaces deterministic browser tests.

roadmap.title: Reduce hourly validation latency
acceptance: Profile current unit and browser suites before editing.
Keep unit runtime below ten seconds and hourly targeted browser validation below three minutes without deleting meaningful regression coverage.
Separate deterministic state tests, smoke paths, visual labs, and release-only full E2E coverage.

notes: The hourly orchestrator must inspect live tasks and branches because this file is updated only after integration.
The hourly integrator may update executionState and task statuses in the same focused commit as successful integration metadata.
